_about: Master inventory of the candidate's experience. The source of truth for the resume tailor. Every claim in any tailored resume MUST trace back to a bullet, skill, or fact in this file. Fill what you have; leave nulls/empty arrays for what you don't. Don't invent.

candidate.name: First Last
candidate.headline: Optional one-line tagline (e.g., 'Backend engineer, payments + infra')
candidate.work_authorization: US citizen / Green card / H1B / etc. — only fill if relevant to JD

summary: Optional 2-3 sentence positioning. Used as a starting point for tailored summaries. If you don't want a summary section, set to null.

experiences.title: Senior Backend Engineer
experiences.context: B2B SaaS, ~80 engineers, ~$50M ARR. Was on the Payments team (4 engineers).

bullets.text: VERBATIM bullet text as you'd say it in a long-form version. The script will reframe this for specific JDs.

skills._note: Only list skills you've actually used in real work. Be conservative — humans reviewing resumes notice padding.

projects.name: Open-source contribution / side project name
projects.context: Optional context (why you built it, what came of it)

preferences._about: Inputs that shape the tailored resume but don't appear on it directly.
preferences.voice: Concise; technical; confident but not boastful. Avoid corporate filler ('synergize', 'leverage at scale').

_user_notes: Free-form notes for yourself or for Claude. Examples: 'My GPA was 3.4 — usually omit unless asked', 'Don't lead with the Acme role for security positions', 'I'm pivoting from payments to infra'.
